abstract: BARRY--Kim, died tragically on November 20, 2004. Kim was a Fellow during 1997-1998 in the Arthur Garfield Hays Civil Liberties Program of New York University School of Law. She was an especially gifted student and performed her duties in the Hays Program with great distinction and extraordinary generosity. It is sad beyond words that Kim cannot continue her altruistic and loving life as a public interest lawyer. We shall never forget her. Norman Dorsen Sylvia A. Law Helen Hershkoff Michael Wishnie Co-Directors, Hays Civil Liberties Program

abstract: The only ones scrambling more frantically than the elderly Americans queuing up for flu shots are the federal health officials who are desperately seeking to direct the nation's truncated supply of vaccine to those who most need it. The sudden loss of almost half of the vaccine doses originally expected for use here has exposed not only the rickety state of the vaccine manufacturing base, but also the weaknesses in the mechanisms for determining who gets the vaccine in a time of dire shortage.    This is no great surprise to experts who follow vaccine issues closely. For the past several years, the Government Accountability Office has deplored the lack of an effective plan for distributing flu vaccine in a time of emergency, or even during a normal flu season when vaccine supplies are disrupted. Just a week before contamination problems at a plant in England blocked the use of some 48 million doses of flu vaccine this year, the G.A.O. prophetically warned Congress that ''there is no system in place to ensure that seniors and others at high risk for complications receive flu vaccinations first when vaccine is in short supply.''

headline.main: Vaccine for Congress and the Bears
headline.print_headline: Vaccine for Congress and the Bears
